title: Commit Message Writer
description: Use when writing a Git commit message from a local diff, change summary, staged files, changelog entry, or release-prep commit.
instructions: Inspect the actual change, not just filenames, when a diff is available.
Choose a Conventional Commit type based on primary impact: feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, chore, perf, build, or ci.
Choose a narrow scope from the affected subsystem, package, route, component, or command; omit scope when it would be generic.
Write an imperative subject under 72 characters, lowercase after the type, with no trailing period.
Add a body only when it explains motivation, compatibility, migration, risk, or validation that the subject cannot carry.
Recommend splitting commits when multiple unrelated changes are present.
Default output is only the ready-to-use commit message; add Notes only when accuracy is uncertain.
Do not use vague subjects like update files, misc fixes, address comments, or wip, and do not claim tests ran without evidence.

examples.input: Added null checks for workspace config loader and tests.
examples.output:
fix(config): guard empty workspace metadata

Prevent loader crash when workspace has no config file.
Add regression tests for empty metadata path.
